Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Apr-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff Glenda Blankenship.
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel, appearing through CourtCall.
Hilda Akopyan of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (213-622-7300), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
Emilie E. De La Motte of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Lamons Gasket Company and Contra Costa Electric, Inc.
Jason Kort of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Great American Insurance Company.
Steven Spaulding of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing through CourtCall for defendant BroadSpectrum
DownStream Services.
Kristi Young of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (415) 512-4381, appearing through CourtCall for defendant Oscar E. Erickson, Inc.
Christine Hawkins of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing through CourtCall for defendant Republic Supply Company.
Charles S. Park of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Foster Wheeler LLC.
Jocelyn M. Soriano of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Riley Power Inc. and Zurn Industries, LLC.
Jennifer Ways of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Consolidated Insulation, Inc.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is July 26, 2021 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is August 6, 2021. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. Plaintiff in its Reply offered to shorten response discovery time to 20 days. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 22, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is July 9, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date July 23, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than May 7, 2021. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Apr-08-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than April 22, 2021, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than April 22, 2021, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on June 9, 2021 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than June 11, 2021. Responses to the demand are due no later than June 25, 2021. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by July 16, 2021 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, July 22, 2021 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Court declined Defendant American Biltrite Inc.'s request to include plaintiff's to provide reports and records and date for plaintiffs' deposition in the order. Parties to meet and confer on these matters. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok.
